message:
test(fuzz): add NodeKeyAttestation sign/verify binding fuzz target (resolves xedeu)

NodeKeyAttestation::sign + verify (identity.rs:302-387) is the
owner-signed Ed25519 attestation that binds (node_id, signing_kid,
encryption_kid, issuance_kid, tags, issued_at, expires_at). The mesh's
identity model relies on this binding — fcp_tags() and
verified_fcp_tags() refuse to surface a node's zone memberships unless
verify returns Ok. A binding regression therefore lets an attacker spoof
zone tags or substitute a key for one they control.

Existing fuzz coverage in fcp-tailscale (tailscale_acl,
tailscale_status_parse) does not touch the attestation surface.

Six MRs:
- round-trip sign(...) → verify(...) succeeds
- tag canonicalization: signing tags=[A,B,C] verifies under reversed
  AND duplicate-bearing tag lists (canonical_tag_strings sort+dedup)
- node-id binding: verify under different node_id → InvalidAttestation
- key-set binding: per-role swap of signing/encryption/issuance verifying
  key → InvalidAttestation
- tag-list binding: verify under tags=[..signed.., +extra] → reject
- owner-key binding: verify under different owner pubkey → reject
  (signer_kid mismatch path at identity.rs:361)

Once-gated regression anchor exercises sort + dedup independently with
hand-constructed tag pairs, plus an acceptance anchor that strictly
different tag sets MUST be rejected (otherwise the canonical-form
checks would be vacuous).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -475,5 +475,12 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_tailscale_attestation_binding"
+path = "fuzz_targets/tailscale_attestation_binding.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [workspace]
 members = ["."]
